title: The 7 1/2 Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle
author: Stuart Turton
comments: Currently reading...

title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Donald Norman
comments: I think this changed my life in a very small way, this book will be at the front of my mind whenever I interact with any new product, and definitely whenever I run into a terribly made product.

title: On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous
author: Ocean Vuong
comments: Striking and honest. I really enjoy when a book offers insight into the lives of others, through the common medium of struggle.

title: How Countries Go Broke
author: Ray Dalio
comments: Lots of incredibly valuable information is in here, giving a very understandable view of global cycles. Unfortunately it's not perfect and Ray Dalio suffers from sounding too much like a 76 year old founder of the largest hedge fund on planet Earth, at least in some sections of the book.

title: Azumanga Daioh Omnibus
author: Kiyohiko Azuma
comments: really enjoyed it ^-^ first manga i have read i think i started watching the anime recently also which also has been fun, i'll be sad when it's all over

title: Letter From Japan
author: Marie Kondo
comments: I have only positive things to say. I won't say what they are.

title: The Bell Jar
author: Sylvia Plath
comments: A poem is nothing like a piece of dust. scarily good literature I wished I had read earlier, but maybe I wouldn't have been fit to read it earlier.

title: Algorithms
author: Panos Louridas
comments: i learned

title: Y2K: How the 2000s Became Everything (essays on the future that never was)
author: Colette Shade
comments: I expected more and also less if that makes sense? I feel I didn't get exactly what I wanted with this book, I feel it didn't go far enough with its points and it's purpose in trying to emphasize how truly important the 2000s were. It's kind of scattered and Colette shade really wants to talk about politics (which isn't an issue but after reading idk if I want to hear colette shade talk about politics anymore). The book still had good information and was fun to read.

title: The Metamorphosis & Other Stories
author: Franz Kafka
comments: I have no doubt in my mind that The Metamorphosis itself is his magnum opus, but it was really interesting reading the other stories in this compilation.. I feel there's a 50% chance I would get along with Kafka, from his stories, I feel he would be a wildcard. Maybe 30%. He would be interesting to talk to.

title: Beautiful Music for Ugly Children
author: Kirstin Cronn-Mills
comments: I liked the part when they mentioned the Decemberists, and the rest of the book too I guess.... an easier read but I enjoyed it. i cried too

title: Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
comments: I didn't know this book was a 'trilogy', I thought it was just one book for the longest time..

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
comments: I've been told the best way to read Leaves of Grass is aloud.. I'll need to try that out sometime

title: Enlightenment Now
author: Steven Pinker
comments: Steven Pinker is a terrible person do not buy his books. The book itself was alright I just wished I didn't give him money

title: I Deliver Parcels in Beijing
author: Hu Anyan
comments: I need to write a blog about this book

title: Socialism: Uptopian and Scientific
author: Friedrich Engels
comments: kind of boring. sorry for the reductive take...

title: The Communist Manifesto
author: Karl Marx
comments: made sure to read this book out loud on public transport with a copy that had a hammer and sickle on the front <- is this funny

title: Fun Home
author: Alison Bechdel
comments: Read this for an lgbtq literature class, and absolutely loved it

title: The Well of Loneliness
author: Radclyffe Hall
comments: also read this for an lgbtq literature class. I want to reread eventually (probably will end up being so long from now) since when I first read it I was under the time constraint of the class. idk it's also just a good book

title: Imre: A Memorandum
author: Edward Prime-Stevenson
comments: Also!!! read this for an lgbtq literature class. I don't have much to say about Imre. This is the only book on here I don't have the hard copy of!

title: Fragments of Sappho
author: Sappho
comments: lesbians wow

title: Seek You: A Journey Through American Loneliness
author: Kristen Radtke
comments: My college gave this one to me, and I really did enjoy it! nonfiction graphic novel about loneliness, which at the time I wasn't aware that I would be reading another nonfiction graphic novel this year. (Fun Home) I hope they give us another free book next year, and while I doubt it, in this case it doesn't hurt to hope
